The old lighthouse stood on the cliff edge, its beam cutting through the thick fog that rolled in from the sea. Captain Morris climbed the spiral stairs slowly, each step echoing in the stone chamber. He reached the top and looked out across the dark waters, searching for any sign of the missing vessel. The storm had been fierce, lasting three full days. Waves had battered the coast without mercy, tearing apart the wooden docks and flooding the lower village streets. Now the silence was almost worse than the howling wind had been. He adjusted the heavy lens and sent the light sweeping across the horizon once more.
